data.title: Germany's retail sales rise 1.9% Y/Y in July, missing estimates
data.description:
• Retail sales in Germany increased 1.90% in July over the same month in the previous year, missing consensus of 2.6%.

  • Retail sales dropped 1.50% in July over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Trump officials say end of low-value package tariff exemption is permanent - report
data.description:
The U.S. tariff exemption for package shipments valued under $800 ends permanently on Friday, with a six-month transition period under which postal service shippers can opt to pay a flat duty of $80 to $200 per package depending on the country of origin, reported Reuters.

The report, which cited Trump administration officials, said the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) agency will begin collecting normal duty rates on all global parcel imports, regardless of value, after 12:01 a.m. EDT (0401 GMT) on Friday.

"President Trump's ending of the deadly de minimis loophole will save thousands of American lives by restricting the flow of narcotics and other dangerous prohibited items, and add up to $10 billion a year in tariff revenues to our Treasury," White House trade adviser Peter Navarro told reporters, according to the report.

The move comes after President Trump in July signed an executive order that expanded the suspension of duty-free de minimis treatment for low-value shipments to a global scale, after suspending the provision for shipments from China and Hong Kong in May.

 The loophole was exploited by fast fashion retailers like SHEIN, as well as Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) to ship packages duty-free from China. As a result of the exemption, the volume of de minimis shipments entering the U.S. exploded from 134M to 1.36B over a 9-year period.

The Reuters report added that based on CBP guidance, parcels would be charged $80 from countries with Trump-imposed duty rates below 16%, such as Britain and the European Union, $160 from countries between 16% and 25%, such as Indonesia and Vietnam, and $200 from countries above 25%, including China, Brazil, India, and Canada.

data.title: Asian indexes turn lower despite record close for Wall Street
data.description:
Asian indexes traded mostly lower on Friday, as investors awaited a key U.S. inflation report and after gains in tech stocks on Wall Street helped propel the market to another all-time high.

Gold hovered around $3,410 per ounce on Friday, near its highest level in over a month, and is on track for a second consecutive weekly gain

Japan (NKY:IND) dropped 0.34% , while Japan unemployment rate dropped to 2.3% in July, retail sales rose. Japan’s housing starts shrank. The Japanese yen held around 147 per dollar on Friday, remaining sideways for nearly four weeks as investors digested a series of economic reports

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.33%, extending the previous session’s advance amid broad market strength. Chinese equities on track for monthly gains on easing US-China trade tensions, expectations of new stimulus, and stronger fund inflows, rising retail participation, and margin buying. 

The offshore yuan weakened to around 7.12 per dollar on Friday, retreating from a more than nine-month high reached in the prior session and snapping a five-day winning streak. 

China has extended anti-dumping duties on phenol imports from the U.S., the EU, South Korea, Japan, and Thailand for an additional five years, effective August 29, the Ministry of Commerce announced in a statement on Thursday.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.51%, rising for the first session in four after Wall Street’s S&P 500 and Dow Jones closed at record highs 

South Korea unveiled a record KRW 728 trillion budget plan for 2026.

Singapore’s bank loans rose to SGD 854.0 billion in July.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.15%, erasing losses from the previous two sessions. Gains were primarily driven by banking and financial services stocks. The BSE Sensex tracked an upbeat session on Wall Street overnight.

The Indian economy remains resilient, supported by strong rural demand, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) said in its monthly bulletin released on Thursday.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.14%, pulling back gains from the previous two-session rally, and on track for its first weekly loss in four weeks. Australia’s private sector credit rose 0.7% M/M in July.

The Australian dollar edged higher to around $0.654 on Friday, marking its fourth consecutive session of gains to hit a two-week high.

In the U.S. on Thursday, Wall Street’s major averages  finished higher, as investors weighed a wave of economic data and Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest earnings, all as the S&P 500 surpassed 6,500 and marked yet another record close. 

U.S. stock futures turned negative on Friday, Dow -0.17%; S&P 500 -0.05%; Nasdaq -0.15%.

 

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Japan unemployment rate drops to 2.3% in July, retail sales rise
data.description:
• Japan’s unemployment rate dropped to 2.3% in July 2025, below market expectations of 2.5% and down from the same level in the previous four months.

  • It was the lowest jobless rate since December 2019, as the number of unemployed fell to 1.64 million in July, the lowest since January 2020.

  • Retail sales in Japan rose by 0.3% Y/Y in July 2025, slowing sharply from a downwardly revised 1.9% gain in the previous month and falling well short of market expectations for a 1.8% increase.

data.title: Capital One to cut 215 jobs at former Discover offices - report
data.description:
Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF) plans to lay off 215 employees based at the former offices of Discover Financial Services, the Washington Business Journal reported.

According to the report, citing Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification with the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, the notice states that 193 positions will be eliminated by Oct. 17, while the remaining 22 are scheduled for termination in May 2026.

Capital One did not immediately respond to a request for comments.

The report further said the layoffs are tied to Capital One's decision to wind down Discover's mortgage business.

data.title: Gamehaus Holdings announces $5 million share repurchase program
data.description:
• Gamehaus Holdings (NASDAQ:GMHS) announced a share repurchase program under which the company may repurchase up to $5 million of its Class A ordinary shares.
  • The program is effective immediately for a one-year period through August 28, 2026.
  • The company expects to fund repurchases under this program with its existing cash balance and cash generated from operations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oak Woods Acquisition receives continued listing compliance notification from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Oak Woods Acquisition (NASDAQ:OAKU) said it has received a written notice from Nasdaq, which indicated that, as a result of the company’s delay in filing its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025, the company was not in compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • The notice states that the company has until October 21, 2025, 60 calendar days from the date of the notification letter, to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OP Bancorp announces new stock repurchase program
data.description:
• OP Bancorp (NASDAQ:OPBK) on Friday announced a new stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase up to 700,000 shares of its common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ambarella raises fiscal 2026 revenue growth target to 31%-35% as edge AI demand accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ambarella (AMBA) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO Feng-Ming Wang opened by highlighting, "Our strong momentum continued in our second quarter with revenue of $95.5 million, increasing 11% sequentially above the high end of our prior guidance range of $86 million to $94 million." He also announced, "With a strong order book as well as our expectation for both our total unit shipped and our average selling price to increase in fiscal 2026, we are increasing our fiscal 2026 revenue growth estimate to a range of 31% to 35% or approximately $379 million at the midpoint."
  • Wang reported, "We continue to see very strong demand for our new 5-nanometer AI SoCs in both our existing and emerging edge AI markets, which is driving our firm-wide average ASP price higher." He detailed three rapidly emerging applications: portable video, robotic aerial drones, and edge infrastructure, noting, "We expect to commence high-volume shipments into the robotics market by the end of this fiscal year."
  • Wang shared major customer wins, such as Arashi Vision launching the Antigravity A1 drone powered by Ambarella’s CV5 AI SoC and the company’s first win in edge AI infrastructure with the N1-655 SoC. Additional wins included Samsara’s AI Multicam platform, Audi and BAIC Stelato S9 for e-mirror functions, and Honeywell’s 50 Series security cameras in India.
  • Wang concluded, "Edge AI is expected to represent about 80% of our total revenue this year."
  • CFO John Young stated, "For fiscal Q2, revenue was $95.5 million, above the high end of our prior guidance range of $86 million to $94 million, up 11.2% from the prior quarter and up 49.9% year-over-year." He added, "Non-GAAP gross margin for fiscal Q2 was 60.5%, at the low end of our prior guidance range of 60.5% to 62% due to product mix."

Outlook

  • Wang announced, "The midpoint of our new third quarter and the full fiscal year 2026 revenue guidance range represents all-time record quarterly and fiscal year total revenue for Ambarella."
  • Young provided, "In Q3, we forecast revenue in the range of $100 million to $108 million or $104 million at the midpoint. Sequentially, we expect mid- to high single-digit percent growth in our automotive business with our IoT business up in the mid-teens. For fiscal 2026, we anticipate a revenue growth range of 31% to 35%."
  • Guidance for fiscal Q3 non-GAAP gross margin is expected to be in the range of 60% to 61.5% and non-GAAP OpEx in the third quarter is anticipated to be in the range of $54 million to $57 million.

Financial Results

  • The company reported, "Q2 net interest and other income was $2.2 million. Q2 non-GAAP tax provision was approximately $200,000. We reported a non-GAAP net profit of $6.4 million or $0.15 per diluted share in Q2."
  • Cash and marketable securities were $261.2 million at quarter end. Receivables days sales outstanding increased from 31 days in the prior quarter to 40 days, and days of inventory decreased from 98 days to 85 days. Operating cash inflow was $5.5 million, capital expenditures were $4.1 million, and free cash flow was $1.4 million.
  • WT Microelectronics represented 71% of revenue for the second quarter.

Q&A

  • Christopher Adam Jackson Rolland, Susquehanna: Questioned whether IoT could become a larger focus than automotive. Wang responded that the company will "continue to focus on those two areas," leveraging hardware architecture across both but acknowledged IoT is currently contributing more to income.
  • Kevin Edward Cassidy, Rosenblatt Securities: Asked about the drone market and process technology. Wang replied, "We will continue to work on the 2-nanometer project and still remain targeted to take our customers to production in early 2027."
  • Shadi Mitwalli, Needham & Company: Asked about Q3 guidance implying a seasonally down Q4. Wang explained, "Some of the products become driven by consumer cycles, that will definitely explain to you, that's why we are seeing the seasonality based on our guidance."
  • Liam Yevgeny Pharr, BofA Securities: Asked about M&A rumors and growth sustainability. Wang stated, "We just have no comment on that," and described continued growth for both IoT and automotive.
  • Kyle Robert Smith, Stifel: Inquired about inventory and customer demand. Wang said, "We haven't seen any inventory build beyond the normal practice."
  • David O'Connor, BNP Paribas: Asked about automotive ADAS adoption and growth drivers. Wang noted OEMs are focusing on "low end Level 2+ than higher end," while Young estimated growth is "roughly 50-50 between ASP and unit growth."
  • Auguste Philip Richard, Northland Capital Markets: Requested IoT split between security and nonsecurity. Wang said portable video is "definitely grow the fastest."
  • Zhihua Yang, Oppenheimer & Co.: Asked if portable video growth is driven by one customer. Wang credited multiple customers but identified Insta360 as the largest.
  • Richard Cutts Shannon, Craig-Hallum: Asked about the edge infrastructure pipeline. Wang confirmed engagement with "multiple customers, new and old or existing customer with potential design wins."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was generally positive, focusing on growth, market opportunities, and design wins, but included probing questions on seasonality, sustainability, and M&A rumors.
  • Management maintained a confident and assertive tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing momentum and strategic execution. During Q&A, Wang and Young provided detailed, occasionally cautious answers, particularly around future automotive growth and inventory checks. Confidence was shown with phrases like, "we are increasing our fiscal 2026 revenue growth estimate."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the sentiment among management was more optimistic and assertive, while analysts remained positive but increasingly focused on the sustainability and sources of growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q2 earnings revealed a higher revenue growth trajectory, with the fiscal 2026 revenue estimate raised from 19%-25% in Q1 to 31%-35% in Q2.
  • The emphasis on portable video, robotics, and edge infrastructure as new growth engines intensified, with more details provided on customer wins and applications.
  • IoT's outperformance relative to automotive was more pronounced, and management reiterated plans to increase resource allocation to IoT while maintaining a focus on automotive long-term.
  • Gross margin guidance was tightened downward slightly due to product mix compared to Q1.
  • Management’s tone shifted from cautiously optimistic in Q1 to overtly confident in Q2, while analysts probed further into long-term sustainability and the potential for market shifts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted seasonality in Q4 due to consumer cycles.
  • No evidence of inventory build-ups was reported, with frequent checks in place.
  • The automotive sector faces slower OEM program decisions and longer time to revenue, with more rapid growth occurring in other edge AI applications.
  • Analyst concerns centered on the sustainability of growth, the impact of customer concentration, and the evolving market split between IoT and automotive.
  • M&A rumors were addressed with no comment from management.

Final Takeaway

Ambarella's Q2 2026 call signaled a significant acceleration in revenue growth expectations, driven by strong demand and expanding applications for edge AI across IoT, robotics, and infrastructure markets. The company raised its fiscal 2026 revenue growth estimate to a range of 31% to 35%, highlighted record edge AI revenue, and confirmed its leadership position in this technology segment. Management underscored ongoing innovation, a robust customer pipeline, and increased resource allocation for high-growth IoT opportunities, while maintaining a long-term commitment to the automotive market and rigorous inventory oversight.
